# logLik method for grpreg

Calculates the log likelihood and degrees of freedom for a fitted grpreg
object.

## Usage

``` r
# S3 method for class 'grpreg'
logLik(object, df.method = c("default", "active"), REML = FALSE, ...)

# S3 method for class 'grpsurv'
logLik(object, df.method = c("default", "active"), ...)
```

## Arguments

- object:

  A fitted `grpreg` or `grpsurv` object, as obtained from
  [`grpreg()`](https://pbreheny.github.io/grpreg/reference/grpreg.md) or
  [`grpsurv()`](https://pbreheny.github.io/grpreg/reference/grpsurv.md)

- df.method:

  How should effective model parameters be calculated? One of:
  `"active"`, which counts the number of nonzero coefficients; or
  `"default"`, which uses the calculated `df` returned by `grpreg`.
  Default is `"default"`.

- REML:

  Use restricted MLE for estimation of the scale parameter in a gaussian
  model? Default is FALSE.

- ...:

  For S3 method compatibility.

## Value

Returns an object of class 'logLik', in this case consisting of a number
(or vector of numbers) with two attributes: 'df' (the estimated degrees
of freedom in the model) and 'nobs' (number of observations).

The 'print' method for 'logLik' objects is not intended to handle
vectors; consequently, the value of the function does not necessarily
display correctly. However, it works with 'AIC' and 'BIC' without any
glitches and returns the expected vectorized output.

## Details

Exists mainly for use with
[`stats::AIC()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/AIC.html) and
[`stats::BIC()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/AIC.html).

## See also

[`grpreg()`](https://pbreheny.github.io/grpreg/reference/grpreg.md)

## Author

Patrick Breheny

## Examples

``` r
data(Birthwt)
X <- Birthwt$X
y <- Birthwt$bwt
group <- Birthwt$group
fit <- grpreg(X,y,group,penalty="cMCP")
logLik(fit) ## Display is glitchy for vectors
```
